name: labelled-path-traversal
pr_title: feat: serve repo docs from the file system
pr_body: New endpoint that streams a docs file by name so the SPA can fetch markdown without a backend rewrite.
diff:
diff --git a/src/docs.py b/src/docs.py
index 1111111..2222222 100644
--- a/src/docs.py
+++ b/src/docs.py
@@ -1,4 +1,12 @@
 from flask import Flask, request
 
 app = Flask(__name__)
+
+
+@app.get("/docs")
+def serve_doc():
+    name = request.args.get("name", "")
+    path = "/var/app/docs/" + name
+    with open(path, "rb") as f:
+        return f.read()
guidelines: Never construct filesystem paths from request parameters by concatenation. Always normalise via pathlib.Path and verify the resolved path is still under the intended root.

expected.note: Path traversal — `name` from the query string is concatenated into a filesystem path with no normalisation. A request for `?name=../../etc/passwd` reads any file the process has access to.
